## Purpose
This file serves as the documentation for DiffDoc, a tool that transforms codebase implementations into a searchable knowledgebase, generating plain-English summaries from source files and maintaining context close to the repository.

## User-Visible Behavior
Users can initialize DiffDoc, create summaries of their code, search through those summaries, and query the knowledgebase for implementation details. The documentation provides command-line instructions and examples for various operations.

## Business Rules
DiffDoc operates under several guiding principles, including treating the codebase as the source of truth, ensuring summaries reflect implemented behavior, and maintaining an evolving knowledgebase that updates with changes in the code.

## Data Inputs And Outputs
Data inputs include command-line arguments, configuration files, and environment variables. Outputs consist of generated summaries in JSON format, a manifest file mapping file paths to content hashes, and a local search index.

## Side Effects
DiffDoc creates or updates configuration files, generates summaries, and builds a local search index. It may also log output and manage file selection rules based on `.diffdocignore`.

## Error And Edge Cases
Potential errors include missing configuration files, invalid command-line arguments, and issues with external model servers. Edge cases may arise from unsupported file types or empty results during searches.

## Dependencies
DiffDoc relies on Node.js and requires an OpenAI-compatible chat model and embedding model for its operations. It can also work with local model servers for processing.

## Operational Notes
DiffDoc supports various configurations through CLI flags, configuration files, and environment variables. It is designed to be run in CI environments and provides options for managing concurrency and embedding batch sizes. Users should be cautious about committing sensitive information and should manage generated files appropriately.
